description:
Context:
    You are a Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) on-call and need to verify that the production host is correctly allowing HTTPS traffic.  
    For this simulated environment the current iptables rule-set of the server has been exported to the plain-text file

        /home/user/current_firewall.txt

    Goal:
    1. Read /home/user/current_firewall.txt and determine how many ACCEPT rules explicitly target destination port 443 (HTTPS).  
       • Count only those rules that simultaneously contain the substrings “ACCEPT” and “dport 443” (spacing may vary).  
       • Ignore commented lines (those starting with “#”) if any appear.  
    2. Write the result to a new file

        /home/user/port443_count.log

    Required log-file format (must match exactly; automated tests are case- and whitespace-sensitive):

        ALLOWED_PORT_443_COUNT=<N>

       where <N> is the integer count with no leading/trailing spaces.  
       The file must contain a single line terminated by a single UNIX newline (␊).

    Constraints:
    • Accomplish the entire task with ONE terminal command of your choice.  
    • Do not edit /home/user/current_firewall.txt.  
    • Do not create any additional files or directories.  
    • Ensure /home/user/port443_count.log is created/overwritten by your command and has the exact required content.

    Success criteria (checked automatically):
    • /home/user/port443_count.log exists.  
    • Its sole line matches the required format and the correct integer.  
    • No other files were modified.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
    Path: /home/user/current_firewall.txt
    Contents (exact):

    :INPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
    :FORWARD ACCEPT [0:0]
    :OUTPUT ACCEPT [0:0]
    -A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80  -j ACCEPT
    -A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 443 -j ACCEPT
    -A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 22  -j ACCEPT
    -A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 443 -j ACCEPT
    -A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 8080 -j DROP
    -A INPUT -p tcp -m tcp --dport 443 -j ACCEPT

    Ground-truth for validation:
    • Correct count of ACCEPT rules for destination port 443 is 3.
    • Therefore the required /home/user/port443_count.log must contain exactly:

        ALLOWED_PORT_443_COUNT=3␊

      (␊ represents a single newline character)

    Permissions:
    • /home/user is writable by the user running the agent.
    • /home/user/current_firewall.txt is read-only.
name: task_000000_e9badc32
